title: Neoadjuvant intent anti-PD-1 immunotherapy for cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma in patients with hematologic malignancy.
abstract: Patients with cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma (CSCC) and concurrent hematologic malignancy are underrepresented in clinical trials of anti-programmed death-1 (PD1) therapy due to concerns about efficacy and safety. The objective of this study is to evaluate clinical outcomes and safety of neoadjuvant anti-PD1 in patients with CSCC and concurrent hematologic malignancy.
trial_name: Cemiplimab in Locally Advanced Cutaneous Squamous Cell Carcinoma: Phase 2, Single-Arm
